title: Simon Property Group Benefits From A Changing Real Estate Model
text: Simon Property Group was originally viewed as a victim of the decline of malls as consumers increasingly moved to online shopping. Mixed-use real estate is growing substantially, and we see it even surpassing online industry growth in some regards. Simon Property Group is well positioned to take advantage.

title: 3 Ultra-High-Yield Dividend Stocks You'll Regret Not Buying at These Prices
text: Recent market volatility and lingering impacts from the pandemic have crushed these 3 dividend REITs.

title: All Treats, No Tricks, Just Blue-Chip REITs
text: The markets may be hopped up on headline data about U.S. GDP growth, but most economists don't expect a rebound of any kind to last. I'm rooting for a smorgasbord of cheaper prices on stocks that are bound to bounce back.

text: These two landlords were slammed by the 2022 market drop, but they are doing just fine and paying fat dividends.

title: Simon Property Group: A Bear Turns Bullish, But Risks Remain
text: After being bearish since the pandemic, I am finally turning bullish on Simon Property Group. Occupancy rates have bounced back close to pre-pandemic levels though base rents remain pressured.

title: Simon Property: Now Is Not The Time To Take The Plunge Yet
text: SPG has continued to come under significant pressure from the recent selloff, as the market further de-rated stocks with consumer discretionary spending exposure. We assess that Simon Property's near-term execution risks are likely reflected in its valuation. But, a worse-than-anticipated recession could compress its valuation further.

title: Simon Property Group: The Mall Is Dead, Long Live The Mall
text: The death of the mall has been greatly exaggerated and this presents a rare opportunity for contrarian investors. Class A malls have been extremely resilient, quickly recovering from the Covid crisis and so far showing good results despite the increased economic uncertainty.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks That Are Dirt Cheap Right Now
text: These stocks have been beaten down, despite strong business results and big opportunities.

title: JCPenney to hire nearly 22,000 employees for the holidays
text: JCPenney said Thursday that it is hiring nearly 22,000 seasonal employees for the holiday season. The department store chain, which came out of bankruptcy in December 2020, is owned by Simon Property Group Inc. SPG, -0.10% and Brookfield Asset Management Inc. BAM, -2.08%.

title: Will Retail REITs Continue to Rebound in the Holiday Season?
text: Retail REITs like O, SPG, KIM and FRT are poised to benefit amid pent-up consumer demand with the waning of the pandemic impact and a favorable job-and-wage growth environment, supporting consumer confidence.

title: Realty Income And Simon Property: Eat The Cake And Keep It Too
text: REIT investors usually get to eat the cake now and don't get to keep it, i.e., they get most of their total returns in the form of current dividends instead. However, recent market corrections have created a situation where REIT investors can eat the cake now and also keep a good part of it at the same time.
